text.title: Code Decomposition Patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns the detector catches before the refactor compounds into history loss or broken callers.

antipattern.symptom: Move list violates the pattern's own constraints (e.g. extract-module pulls out I/O code).
antipattern.root-cause: Pattern picked from file name rather than from symbol kinds inside; agent skipped the detect_pattern step.
antipattern.fix: Reject the move list. Re-run detect_pattern on the actual symbol table; require the agent to cite the pattern's trigger condition that matched.

antipattern.symptom: Single commit moves 40+ symbols across 8 files; test command not run in between.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent optimises for one big commit instead of incremental green-test sequence.
antipattern.fix: Reject the PR; require one commit per symbol cluster with the test command captured in the commit message.

antipattern.symptom: Downstream files import the symbol from the old path; CI red at import-time.
antipattern.root-cause: The original file no longer re-exports the moved symbol; rule public-api-stable was skipped.
antipattern.fix: Reinstate the re-export at the original path; defer the API break to a follow-up PR.

antipattern.symptom: `git log --follow` on the moved file shows only the rename commit; pre-move history is detached.
antipattern.root-cause: Delete + create instead of `git mv`; or content edited in the same commit as the rename.
antipattern.fix: Redo the move with `git mv` and split the rename + content edit into two commits.
